schemaorg_recipe: {"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Recipe","name":"MANY TYPES OF WILD BERRIES IN A BROTH OF HERBS AND GRASSES","recipeYield":"4","recipeIngredient":["16 lemon balm tips","16 angelica flowers","16 oregano flowers","16 wild geranium flowers","1 ounce/25 g wheatgrass, roughly chopped","1/2 ounce/13 g fresh dill","1/2 cup/35 g parsley leaves","2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons/40 g Stock syrup (page 256)","2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons/40 g verjus","3/4 ounce/22 g parsley purée (page 247)","4 thimbleberries","8 wild raspberries","8 trailing blackberries","8 salmonberries","12 red huckleberries","16 redcurrants","4 Indian plums","4 gooseberries","4 blueberries","4 salal berries","4 aronia berries","Parsley oil (page 242)"],"recipeInstructions":["In a blender, combine 3/4 cup/190 g water with the wheatgrass, dill, parsley leaves, and stock syrup and blend on high until the liquid is puréed, with just small bits of herbs, about 15 seconds. extracted_ingredients: 2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons/40 g Stock syrup (page 256)
extracted_instructions: In a blender, combine 3/4 cup/190 g water with the wheatgrass, dill, parsley leaves, and stock syrup and blend on high until the liquid is puréed, with just small bits of herbs, about 15 seconds. Pass the liquid through a Superbag and skim the foam from the top of the strained liquid. Season with the verjus (you may need a little more or less than the called-for amount) and stir in the parsley purée to thicken the mixture.

Arrange a mix of berries in the bottom of each chilled serving bowl. Drizzle about a tablespoon of the grass broth and a few drops of parsley oil over the berries and garnish with the herbs and flowers.
